item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
The image that was used in this article could not be sourced, and thus is assumed to be non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0). As such, the image has been removed.

EDIT: The image was taken by the author and is cc-compliant.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yep, I'm no longer eating gummy bears anymore.

The document never explicitly mentioned it, but can you distinguish them from normal gummy bears by their motion? Do they even move when viewed?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I came here thanks to Great Hippo's 001.

I upvoted because "gumi beeps" makes me laugh like I was in frickin kindergarten.

Plus tardigrades, and plus SCPs are perfectly within their rights to be something that isn't spectacularly freaky, but just an oddity.

I'm glad to see the aforementioned 001-recommended procedure is accurately reflected.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm afraid this one does nothing for me. It's well-written, yes, and imaginative, but it basically boils down to "It's candy that's actually gross bugs and it tastes gross and they nibble your insides on the way down!" It reminds me of a Series I article from back in the day, but not in a good way. The bit with the mysterious, shadowy company peddling these things was the nail in the coffin. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I know this is an old post, but I'll reply for the sake of people discovering this SCP nowadays and asking themselves the same question: the fact that they can not only survive being sliced to bits, but digestion as well. That's not a thing that any life-form does.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's funny. Before this article, I read another one (SCP-1234) that also had a rather goofy "mundane adorable thing turns horrible" theme but whereas that one didn't work for me, this one really does. The writing seems to be much better and makes the SCP more intriguing.

I have a couple observations to make:

It is unknown how they determine the sex of other individuals. After mating, the male dies and is eaten by the female, which gives live birth to 200-300 offspring.

If the SCP doesn't know how the bears determine the sex of individuals, how do the researchers know which ones are male and which ones are female? Or is the Foundation for some reason surprised that the bears are capable of recognizing the sex of members of their own species?

The constant cannibalism serves to limit population growth, resulting in a net gain of zero individuals each mating cycle.

I find this bit especially interesting as it turns out that the infamous cannibalism in praying mantises only occurs in captivity due to the scarcity of food. Makes me wonder if this behaviour would remain if the bears were kept in larger containment units or fed more thoroughly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Where's the image from?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: To cover my own ass for the Credit-A-Thon, just letting you know this image is my own. It used to be on my Twitpic but I took it down from there a long while ago.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-1773

I saw what you did there.
Tardigrades were first discovered in the year 1773.

Very funny. Very scary article. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very nicely done. Got here via the Leak Post that explains it (spoilers inside!).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I quite like this one, but before I can upvote—"the bacteria that causes stomach ulcers" is Heliobacter pylori. Given scientific tone, you may want to include the actual species name somewhere.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: fixed

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Absolutely love the idea, but that second description paragraph's got some messed up tone there (devour each other alive, strongest survive, squirm about, etc)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "Young" is fine. Offspring can be a good replacement but this is just niticky.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
One sentence itches me a bit:

… which gives birth to 200-300 young.

"Young" is not a noun. I can't really put my finger on what it's supposed to be, considering most ways to refer to offspring containing the adjective "young" are out of tone for an SCP article.

Also, it kind of lacks the deciding oomph that settles the matter of upvoting or downvoting. I'm going to leave it as a no-vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Mundane genetic engineering could reasonably (IE, within the rules of SCIENCE!) produce something like this, which is I think along the lines of what Gene meant.

I now return you to shorter, non-sleep-deprived sentences.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The origin of them? The fact that based on the picture they are pretty much indistinguishable from gummy bears? Mimicry is one thing, looking and smelling exactly like what its mimicking is another. Also not EVERYTHING is anomalous, the bonehive(439) is just a parasite that would freak people right the fuck out but has nothing anomalous. Its just the foundation protecting people from strange, but not always impossible, things.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This isn't an SCP. It's just an article on Waterbears. Downvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: They're not really.. bugs, either… It's as much like eating "bugs" as much as eating woodlice is. Although that probably doesn't help either.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What's really anomalous about this? A species of tardigrades possessing mimicry and being host to bacteria.
The beasts should be studied by normal biologists and whatever pranksters pulled this off should face justice.
Downvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wasn't saying otherwise.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: We need more water bear SCPs

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is effing brilliant, I love it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: SCP-1773 is totally different.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: SCP-157, which also tries to get people to eat it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What's the other one?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Two.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: how many tardigrade SCPs do we have, now?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That does NOT make it better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: They don't taste like fruit, they only smell like it to attract predators.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you for ruining my favorite candy for me. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't know about you, but a) eating bugs, b) that taste like fruit (which in itself is all kinds of icky if you ask me) and c) survive being eaten while d) taking bites out of your stomach lining (and who knows, your colon) on the way through your digestive tract is more than enough squick for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Intriguing and thanks for reminding me what a badass organism a Tardigrade is but it needs more squick than "a sugary bug that gives you stomach ulcers."
How about consuming gummi beeps temporarily gives you the extremophile properties of water bears (such as resistance to large doses of radiation, being revived from being frozen at cryonic temperatures) but at a terrible price to your bowels. For example an D-class could survive being locked in the reactor room of Fukushima to do important work but only for so long until he shat out his guts (with gummi beeps still squirming alive in the unholy mess!)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upvoted. I was eating gummi bears when I read this…
so…
thanks for that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1. Not extreme…just weird enough that it needs to be contained. And on the aroma…maybe it's to help them reach new locations? I can see it now:
*customer buys some, starts eating on way home* "oh god this tastes like ass!" *throws it away*

Just a thought.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I figured it would be a Gourmet Terrorist Organisation called Are We Edible Yet. Anyway, no going insane, no death by ass-devouring beings from the Nth Dimension, just some random weirdness. I love it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it, well done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: AWOIY?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this one, it doesn't need to try too hard and doesn't compel anyone to do anything. And someone felt the need to stuff them into bags like gummi bears.

And, thank you so much for not making that someone Are We Overdone Ironically Yet

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This but I have only three positions, Upvote, Downvote, and haven't read, so yeah Downvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not bad, just doesn't do anything for me, no vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is awesome.
